channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: AWS completes the second GDV community audit with participant insurers in Germany
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Amazon Web Services (AWS) has completed its second GDV (German Insurance Association) community audit with 36 members from the Germany insurance industry participating, corresponding to...

item.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
item.description: In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.

item.title: Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security
item.description: Anthropic, AWS, Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI fund the Linux Foundation’s long-term security initiatives focused on open source software. The post Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security appeared firs...

item.title: UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms
item.description: The government agency confirmed the vulnerability could have been exploited to obtain company details and alter records. The post UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Warlock Ransomware Group Augments Post-Exploitation Activities
item.description: In a recent attack, the group showcased stealthier cross-network activity, thanks to its use of a new BYOVD technique and other tools.

item.title: RondoDox botnet expands arsenal targeting 174 flaws, and hits 15,000 daily exploit attempts
item.description: RondoDox botnet targets 174 flaws, reaching 15,000 daily exploit attempts in a more focused and strategic campaign. RondoDox botnet is ramping up attacks, targeting 174 vulnerabilities with up to 15,000 daily exploita...

item.title: 'CursorJack’ Attack Path Exposes Code Execution Risk in AI Development Environment
item.description: CursorJack shows how malicious MCP deeplinks in Cursor IDE can trigger user-approved code execution

item.title: CTG unveils cyber resilience scoring dashboard for measurable risk reduction
item.description: CTG has announced the launch of a cyber resilience scoring dashboard that helps organizations quantify risk, prioritize remediation, and track measurable improvement over time. The dashboard is part of the Group’s Ceg...

item.title: LeakNet Ransomware Uses ClickFix via Hacked Sites, Deploys Deno In-Memory Loader
item.description: The ransomware operation known as LeakNet has adopted the ClickFix social engineering tactic delivered through compromised websites as an initial access method. The use of ClickFix, where users are tricked into manual...

item.title: SCW Trust Agent: AI tracks AI influence in code to reduce software risk
item.description: Secure Code Warrior has announced SCW Trust Agent: AI, a governance solution designed to make AI influence in software development visible, attributable, and enforceable at the point of commit, enabling enterprises to...

item.title: Surf AI Raises $57 Million for Agentic Security Operations Platform
item.description: The company has announced its launch, backed by funding from Accel, Cyberstarts, and Boldstart Ventures. The post Surf AI Raises $57 Million for Agentic Security Operations Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek .

item.title: Xona Systems brings real-time threat response to OT remote access sessions
item.description: Xona Systems has introduced Active Defense, a new capability that enables organizations to stop threats during live remote access sessions in operational technology (OT) environments automatically, without waiting for...

item.title: XM Cyber advances AI security with enhanced exposure and attack path visibility
item.description: XM Cyber has enhanced its Continuous Exposure Management Platform to help organizations adopt AI without exposing themselves to new and emerging threats. The release introduces three major capabilities that enable org...

item.title: Top 5 Things CISOs Need to Do Today to Secure AI Agents
item.description: AI agents are autonomous actors with real access to data and systems, not just copilots. Token Security explains why identity-based access control is critical to prevent misuse and data exposure. [...]

item.title: New font-rendering trick hides malicious commands from AI tools
item.description: A new font-rendering attack causes AI assistants to miss malicious commands shown on webpages by hiding them in seemingly harmless HTML. [...]

item.title: SailPoint improves visibility and control over unauthorized AI use
item.description: SailPoint has announced the launch of SailPoint Shadow AI Remediation, the latest component of its real-time AI governance and security framework. This solution enables organizations to discover, monitor, and secure t...

item.title: Pindrop Fraud Assist uses AI to analyze calls and strengthen fraud prevention
item.description: Pindrop has announced Pindrop Protect Fraud Assist, the agentic fraud investigation and case management solution for real-time phone conversations. As AI fraud attacks surge across contact centers, Pindrop Protect Fra...

item.title: Huntress adds tools to its Agentic Security Platform to detect, fix, and prevent endpoint and identity risks
item.description: Huntress has announced Managed Endpoint Security Posture Management (ESPM) and Managed Identity Security Posture Management (ISPM), expanding its Agentic Security Platform to deliver end-to-end protection across endpo...

item.title: Robotic Surgery Giant Intuitive Discloses Cyberattack
item.description: The company says some of its internal business applications were accessed after an employee fell victim to a phishing attack. The post Robotic Surgery Giant Intuitive Discloses Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWe...

item.title: Kore.ai debuts Agent Management Platform to govern enterprise AI ecosystems
item.description: Kore.ai has announced the launch of its Agent Management Platform, a unified command center designed to govern, monitor, and manage AI agents and AI systems across the enterprise. As organizations deploy AI and multi-...

item.title: New Kingston IronKey USB drive delivers enterprise-grade data security
item.description: Kingston Digital has announced the launch of the next-generation IronKey Locker+ 50 G2 (LP50 G2) hardware-encrypted USB flash drive. The drive provides enterprise-grade security with FIPS 197 and AES 256-bit hardware...

item.title: From Windows to macOS: ClickFix attacks shift tactics with ChatGPT-based lures
item.description: ClickFix campaigns are evolving, with attackers increasingly targeting macOS users and deploying more advanced infostealers, according to Sophos researchers. ClickFix is a growing social engineering technique that tri...

item.title: Attack on Stryker’s Microsoft environment wiped employee devices without malware
item.description: The recent cyberattack on Stryker wiped tens of thousands of employee devices through its Microsoft environment, and systems are still offline. A recent cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker targeted its int...

item.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited Wing FTP Vulnerability Leaking Server Paths
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added a medium-severity security flaw impacting Wing FTP to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active explo...

item.title: Runtime malware detection for AWS Fargate
item.description: Application platform modernization is pushing cloud security teams to increasingly support infrastructure teams in new ways as they adopt new cloud architectures. One avenue that is gaining steam is the use of serverl...

item.title: Detecting CVE-2026-3288 & CVE-2026-24512: Ingress-nginx configuration injection vulnerabilities for Kubernetes
item.description: On March 9, 2026, the Kubernetes ingress-nginx project merged a fix for CVE-2026-3288 (CVSS 8.8 HIGH), a configuration injection vulnerability in the NGINX Ingress Controller. The vulnerability allows any user with pe...

item.title: Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment
item.description: Iranian threat group Boggy Serpens' cyberespionage evolves with AI-enhanced malware and refined social engineering. Unit 42 details their persistent targeting. The post Boggy Serpens Threat Assessment appeared first o...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a flaw in Wing FTP Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in Wing FTP Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a...

item.title: Russia-linked APT uses DRILLAPP backdoor to spy on Ukrainian targets
item.description: Russia-linked threat actors target Ukrainian entities with DRILLAPP backdoor and use Edge debugging for stealth. A new DRILLAPP backdoor campaign targets Ukrainian organizations, abusing Microsoft Edge debugging to ev...

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Evolves to Hide in Dependencies
item.description: Researchers have identified dozens of malicious GlassWorm extensions that come with new evasion techniques.

item.title: Inside Olympic Cybersecurity: Lessons From Paris 2024 to Milan Cortina 2026
item.description: Discover how Franz Regul, former CISO for the Paris 2024 Olympics, tackled unique cybersecurity challenges to protect the Games from evolving threats.

item.title: GlassWorm Attack Uses Stolen GitHub Tokens to Force-Push Malware Into Python Repos
item.description: The GlassWorm malware campaign is being used to fuel an ongoing attack that leverages the stolen GitHub tokens to inject malware into hundreds of Python repositories. "The attack targets Python projects — including Dj...

item.title: Stryker attack wiped tens of thousands of devices, no malware needed
item.description: Last week's cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker was limited to its internal Microsoft environment and remotely wiped tens of thousands of employee devices. [...]

item.title: Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization
item.description: The evolution of Iranian cyber operations in broad context: from custom wiper malware to misuse of legitimate admin tools and more. The post Iranian Cyber Threat Evolution: From MBR Wipers to Identity Weaponization ap...

item.title: Standing up for the open Internet: why we appealed Italy’s "Piracy Shield" fine
item.description: Cloudflare is appealing a €14 million fine from Italian regulators over "Piracy Shield," a system that forces providers to block content without oversight. We are challenging this framework to protect the Internet fro...

item.title: Oracle EBS Hack: Only 4 Corporate Giants Still Silent on Potential Impact
item.description: Broadcom, Bechtel, Estée Lauder, and Abbott Technologies are the only major companies that have yet to issue a public statement. The post Oracle EBS Hack: Only 4 Corporate Giants Still Silent on Potential Impact appea...

item.title: FBI launches inquiry into Steam games spreading malware
item.description: The FBI is asking gamers who installed malware-infected Steam games between May 2024 and January 2026 to come forward as part of an ongoing investigation. The FBI is seeking gamers who downloaded Steam games later fou...

item.title: Researchers Warn of Global Surge in Fake Shipment Tracking Scams
item.description: Some of these campaigns are linked to Darcula, a Chinese-language phishing-as-a-service platform

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Chrome 0-Days, Router Botnets, AWS Breach, Rogue AI Agents & More
item.description: Some weeks in security feel normal. Then you read a few tabs and get that immediate “ah, great, we’re doing this now” feeling. This week has that energy. Fresh messes, old problems getting sharper, and research that s...

item.title: Attackers Abuse LiveChat to Phish Credit Card, Personal Data
item.description: A social engineering campaign impersonating PayPal and Amazon uses customer support interactions to acquire sensitive info.

item.title: Shadow AI is everywhere. Here’s how to find and secure it.
item.description: Shadow AI is quietly spreading across SaaS environments as employees adopt new AI tools without IT oversight. Nudge Security explains how security teams can discover AI apps, monitor usage, and govern risky AI activit...

item.title: CrackArmor Flaws Expose Linux Systems to Privilege Escalation
item.description: CrackArmor AppArmor flaws let local Linux users gain root, break containers and enable DoS attacks

item.title: Possible New Result in Quantum Factorization
item.description: I’m skeptical about—and not qualified to review—this new result in factorization with a quantum computer, but if it’s true it’s a theoretical improvement in the speed of factoring large numbers with a quantum computer.

item.title: Critical n8n Flaws Allow Remote Code Execution and Exposure of Stored Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two now-patched security flaws in the n8n workflow automation platform, including two critical bugs that could result in arbitrary command execution. The vulnerabili...

item.title: BlackSanta EDR-Killer Targets HR Teams in CV-Themed Campaign
item.description: BlackSanta malware targets HR staff with fake resumes, kills EDR and steals system data

item.title: Researchers Discover Major Security Gaps in LLM Guardrails
item.description: Palo Alto Networks’ Unit 42 has developed a successful attack to bypass safety guardrails in popular generative AI tools

item.title: Meta Disables 150K Accounts Linked to Southeast Asia Scam Centers in Global Crackdown
item.description: Meta on Wednesday said it disabled over 150,000 accounts associated with scam centers in Southeast Asia as part of a coordinated effort in partnership with authorities from Thailand, the U.S., the U.K., Canada, Korea,...

item.title: Slashing agent token costs by 98% with RFC 9457-compliant error responses
item.description: Cloudflare now returns RFC 9457-compliant structured Markdown and JSON error payloads to AI agents, replacing heavyweight HTML pages with machine-readable instructions. This reduces token usage by over 98%, turning br...

item.title: AI Security for Apps is now generally available
item.description: Cloudflare AI Security for Apps is now generally available, providing a security layer to discover and protect AI-powered applications, regardless of the model or hosting provider. We are also making AI discovery free...

item.title: Es ist offiziell: Wiz wird Teil von Google
item.description: Wir begrüßen eine neue Ära der Cloud- und KI-Sicherheit.

item.title: Microsoft Fixes Two Publicly Disclosed Zero-Days
item.description: March Patch Tuesday sees Microsoft release updates for 79 flaws

item.title: Microsoft Patches 84 Flaws in March Patch Tuesday, Including Two Public Zero-Days
item.description: Microsoft on Tuesday released patches for a set of 84 new security vulnerabilities affecting various software components, including two that have been listed as publicly known. Of these, eight are rated Critical, and...

item.title: UNC6426 Exploits nx npm Supply-Chain Attack to Gain AWS Admin Access in 72 Hours
item.description: A threat actor known as UNC6426 leveraged keys stolen following the supply chain compromise of the nx npm package last year to completely breach a victim's cloud environment within a span of 72 hours. The attack start...

item.title: Middle East Conflict Highlights Cloud Resilience Gaps
item.description: Data centers — used by both governments and militaries for operations — are now fair game, not just for cyberattacks, but for kinetic attacks as well.

item.title: 'Overly Permissive' Salesforce Cloud Configs in the Crosshairs
item.description: Some customers have mishandled guest user configurations otherwise intended to allow third-party access to important — and sensitive — client data.

item.title: AWS European Sovereign Cloud achieves first compliance milestone: SOC 2 and C5 reports plus seven ISO certifications
item.description: In January 2026, we announced the general availability of the AWS European Sovereign Cloud, a new, independent cloud for Europe entirely located within the European Union (EU), and physically and logically separate fr...

item.title: Russian Threat Actor Sednit Resurfaces With Sophisticated Toolkit
item.description: After several years of using simple implants, the Russia-affiliated actor is back with two new sophisticated malware tools.

item.title: Cloud Attackers Now Prefer Vulnerability Exploits Over Credentials, Google Cloud Finds
item.description: Google Cloud report details a sharp rise in attackers exploiting software vulnerabilities, including React2Shell

item.title: Ericsson Breach Exposes Data of 15k Employees and Customers
item.description: Ericsson data breach affects 15k employees/customers after third-party service provider compromise

item.title: AWS Security Hub is expanding to unify security operations across multicloud environments
item.description: After talking with many customers, one thing is clear: the security challenge has not gotten easier. Enterprises today operate across a complex mix of environments, including on-premises infrastructure, private data c...

item.title: 'BlackSanta' EDR Killer Targets HR Workflows
item.description: A campaign by Russian-speaking cyberattackers hijacks workflows to deliver security-busting malware, allowing attackers to steal data without detection.

item.title: New "LeakyLooker" Flaws in Google Looker Studio Could Enable Cross-Tenant SQL Queries
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed nine cross-tenant vulnerabilities in Google Looker Studio that could have permitted attackers to run arbitrary SQL queries on victims' databases and exfiltrate sensitive data w...

item.title: Investigating multi-vector attacks in Log Explorer
item.description: Log Explorer customers can now identify and investigate multi-vector attacks. Log Explorer supports 14 additional Cloudflare datasets, enabling users to have a 360-degree view of their network.

item.title: Building a security overview dashboard for actionable insights
item.description: Cloudflare's new Security Overview dashboard transforms overwhelming security data into prioritized, actionable insights, empowering defenders with contextual intelligence on vulnerabilities.

item.title: Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Controls
item.description: Unit 42 research reveals AI judges are vulnerable to stealthy prompt injection. Benign formatting symbols can bypass security controls. The post Auditing the Gatekeepers: Fuzzing "AI Judges" to Bypass Security Control...

item.title: ShinyHunters Targets Hundreds of Websites in New Salesforce Campaign
item.description: Prolific ShinyHunters group claims to have stolen data from nearly 400 websites in Experience Cloud attacks

item.title: Threat Actors Mass-Scan Salesforce Experience Cloud via Modified AuraInspector Tool
item.description: Salesforce has warned of an increase in threat actor activity that's aimed at exploiting misconfigurations in publicly accessible Experience Cloud sites by making use of a customized version of an open-source tool cal...

item.title: CISA Flags SolarWinds, Ivanti, and Workspace One Vulnerabilities as Actively Exploited
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added three security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability l...

item.title: Translating risk insights into actionable protection: leveling up security posture with Cloudflare and Mastercard
item.description: Cloudflare will be integrating Mastercard’s RiskRecon attack surface intelligence capabilities to help you eliminate Internet-facing blind spots while continuously monitoring and closing security gaps.

item.title: March 2026 Patch Tuesday: Eight Critical Vulnerabilities and Two Publicly Disclosed Among 82 CVEs Patched
Uncovering agent logging gaps in Copilot Studio
item.description: During research, we sometimes encounter scenarios that remind us that it's a good idea to trust but verify. In September 2025, we noticed that certain Microsoft Copilot Studio agent settings did not log certain admini...

item.title: White House Cyber Strategy Prioritizes Offense
item.description: In a seven-page strategy document, the Trump administration signaled a shift to preemption and deterrence to handling cyber threats.

item.title: 'InstallFix' Attacks Spread Fake Claude Code Sites
item.description: A fresh cyberattack campaign blends malvertising with a ClickFix-style technique that highlights risky behavior with AI coding assistants and command-line interfaces.

item.title: Are We Ready for Auto Remediation With Agentic AI?
item.description: With the rapid innovations in AI, we are entering an exciting era of automated risk remediation. Learn about security team readiness to leverage agentic AI for threat and exposure management.

item.title: Malicious npm Package Posing as OpenClaw Installer Deploys RAT, Steals macOS Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious npm package that masquerades as an OpenClaw installer to deploy a remote access trojan (RAT) and steal sensitive data from compromised hosts. The package, named "@...

item.title: Threat Actor Exploits Flaws and Uses Elastic Cloud SIEM to Manage Stolen Data
item.description: Huntress researchers uncover campaign exploiting vulnerabilities to steal data using Elastic Cloud as a data hub

item.title: Trump Administration Unveils New Cyber Strategy for America
item.description: US national cyber strategy focuses on stronger defenses, countering threats, fostering innovation

item.title: UNC4899 Breached Crypto Firm After Developer AirDropped Trojanized File to Work Device
item.description: The North Korean threat actor known as UNC4899 is suspected to be behind a sophisticated cloud compromise campaign targeting a cryptocurrency organization in 2025 to steal millions of dollars in cryptocurrency. The ac...

item.title: Active defense: introducing a stateful vulnerability scanner for APIs
item.description: Cloudflare’s new Web and API Vulnerability Scanner helps teams proactively find logic flaws. By using AI to build API call graphs, we identify vulnerabilities that standard defensive tools miss.

item.title: UK Launches New Crackdown Unit to Tackle Cyber-Fraud at the Source
item.description: New UK Online Crime Centre will combine expertise from a range of sources to takedown online channels cyber-scammers rely on

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Qualcomm 0-Day, iOS Exploit Chains, AirSnitch Attack & Vibe-Coded Malware
item.description: Another week in cybersecurity. Another week of "you've got to be kidding me." Attackers were busy. Defenders were busy. And somewhere in the middle, a whole lot of people had a very bad Monday morning. That's kind of...

item.title: AI Security Startups Dominate New Cyber Innovation Awards
item.description: Over one in five winners of IT-Harvest’s 2026 Cyber 150 are AI security companies

item.title: Command Your Cloud at Mach Speed: Find Orca Security at RSAC 2026
item.description: RSAC is one of our favorite weeks of the year. A chance to get off Zoom, get into the same room, and have the kinds of conversations that actually move the needle on cloud security. This year, we’re heading to San Fra...

item.title: Chinese Cyber Threat Lurks In Critical Asian Sectors for Years
item.description: An undefined Chinese-speaking actor wields a combo of custom malware, open source tools, and LOTL binaries against Windows and Linux, likely for spying.

item.title: CISA Adds Three Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2021-22054 Omnissa Workspace ONE Server-Side Request Forgery CVE-2025-26399...

item.title: Can the Security Platform Finally Deliver for the Mid-Market?
item.description: Mid-market organizations are constantly striving to achieve security levels on a par with their enterprise peers. With heightened awareness of supply chain attacks, your customers and business partners are defining th...

item.title: New Attack Against Wi-Fi
item.description: It’s called AirSnitch : Unlike previous Wi-Fi attacks, AirSnitch exploits core features in Layers 1 and 2 and the failure to bind and synchronize a client across these and higher layers, other nodes, and other network...

item.title: TriZetto Provider Solutions Breach Hits 3.4 Million Patients
item.description: Billing services provider TriZetto Provider Solutions has begun notifying millions of patients about a data breach

item.title: From Narrative to Knowledge Graph | LLM-Driven Information Extraction in Cyber Threat Intelligence
item.description: LLMs can turn CTI narratives into structured intelligence at scale, but speed-accuracy trade-offs demand careful design for operational defense workflows.

item.title: Ghanaian Pleads Guilty to Role in $100m Romance Scam
item.description: Derrick Van Yeboah admitted he stole over $10m in romance scams as part of crime gang

item.title: Complexity is a choice. SASE migrations shouldn’t take years.
item.description: Discover how Cloudflare partners TachTech and Adapture are shattering the 18-month migration myth, deploying agile SASE for global enterprises in weeks by treating security as software.

item.title: Malware detection with Sysdig
item.description: When we talk about cloud security, we spend a lot of time discussing things like configuration and identity risk. But we shouldn't lose sight of other types of risk, simply because they don't lead to the lion's share...

item.title: Behind the console: Active phishing campaign targeting AWS console credentials
item.description: Datadog Security Research identified an active adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) phishing campaign targeting AWS Console credentials via typosquatted domains that mimic AWS infrastructure.

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. B...

item.title: Cylake Offers AI-Native Security Without Relying on Cloud Services
item.description: Cylake's platform will analyze security data locally and identify potential attacks for organizations concerned about data sovereignty.

item.title: North Korean APTs Use AI to Enhance IT Worker Scams
item.description: DPRK worker scams are old hat, but they're still working, thanks to AI tools that help with everything from face swapping to daily emails.

item.title: EU Auto Rules Shift Gears on Cybersecurity Standards
item.description: The European Union is taking new precautions as climate change and cybersecurity threats rise across the automotive industry.

item.title: From the endpoint to the prompt: a unified data security vision in Cloudflare One
item.description: Cloudflare One unifies data security from endpoint to prompt: RDP clipboard controls, operation-mapped logs, on-device DLP, and Microsoft 365 Copilot scanning via API CASB.

item.title: Proactive Preparation and Hardening Against Destructive Attacks: 2026 Edition
item.description: Written by: Matthew McWhirt, Bhavesh Dhake, Emilio Oropeza, Gautam Krishnan, Stuart Carrera, Greg Blaum, Michael Rudden UPDATE (March 13): Added guidance around abuse or misuse of endpoint / MDM platforms . Background...

item.title: Cyberattack on Mexico's Gov't Agencies Highlight AI Threat
item.description: Using Anthropic's Claude, OpenAI's ChatGPT, and a detailed playbook prompt, a handful of cyberattackers reportedly gained access to government agencies and its citizens' data.

item.title: Introducing Wiz Tenant Manager: Multi-Tenant Management for Federated Organizations
item.description: Experience full Wiz security with zero friction, managing multiple tenants in a single console

item.title: Zero‑Day Attacks on Enterprise Software Reach Record High, Google Warns
item.description: Almost a quarter of the zero days detected by Google in 2025 targeted security and networking appliances

item.title: The Agile FedRAMP Playbook, Part 4: Reactive Risk Management through Enriched Incident Response
item.description: In the final part of our series, we explore Reactive Risk Management. Discover how Wiz for U.S. Government transforms cloud detection and response to help satisfy FedRAMP Rev 5 IR controls and FedRAMP 20x detection be...

item.title: An Investigation Into Years of Undetected Operations Targeting High-Value Sectors
item.description: In-depth analysis of threat activity we call CL-UNK-1068. We discuss their toolset, including tunneling, reconnaissance and credential theft. The post An Investigation Into Years of Undetected Operations Targeting Hig...

item.title: Patch diff to SYSTEM
item.description: Leveraging LLMs and patch diffing, this research details a Use-After-Free vulnerability in Windows DWM, demonstrating a reliable exploit that achieves escalation from low-privileged user permissions to SYSTEM.

item.title: Nation-State Actor Embraces AI Malware Assembly Line
item.description: Pakistan's APT36 threat group has begun using vibe-coding to churn out mediocre malware, but at a scale that could overwhelm defenses.

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Goes Boom as Europol, Vendors Bust Phishing Platform
item.description: The phishing-as-a-service platform was popular among cyber threat actors because of its ability to bypass multifactor authentication defenses.

item.title: Cisco Drops 48 New Firewall Vulnerabilities, 2 Critical
item.description: Edge bugs are so fetch, and Cisco just patched 50 new ones, including some heavy hitters with 10 out of 10 scores on the CVSS scale.

item.title: Patch, track, repeat: The 2025 CVE retrospective
item.description: Thor analyzes CVE data from 2025 and provides recommendations for where and how organizations should strengthen their defenses.

item.title: AWS completes the 2026 annual Dubai Electronic Security Centre (DESC) certification audit
item.description: We’re excited to announce that Amazon Web Services (AWS) has completed the annual Dubai Electronic Security Centre (DESC) certification audit to operate as a Tier 1 Cloud Service Provider (CSP) for the AWS Middle East...

item.title: Fig Security Emerges From Stealth to Fix Broken Security Operations
item.description: Fig Security's platform traces security data flows end-to-end across SIEMs, pipelines, and response systems to alert teams before infrastructure changes break critical defenses.

item.title: AI-Driven Insider Risk Now a “Critical Business Threat,” Report Warns
item.description: Malicious insiders are using misusing AI for nefarious gain, while employees cutting corners also creates risk, warns Mimecast

item.title: Ending the "silent drop": how Dynamic Path MTU Discovery makes the Cloudflare One Client more resilient
item.description: The Cloudflare One Client now features the ability to actively probe and adjust packet sizes. This update eliminates the problems caused by tunnel layering and MTU differences, providing more stability and resiliency.

item.title: LatAm Now Faces 2x More Cyberattacks Than US
item.description: Much of Central and South America struggles with cybersecurity maturity, and hackers are taking advantage.

item.title: ContextCrush Flaw Exposes AI Development Tools to Attacks
item.description: Critical flaw "ContextCrush" in Context7 MCP Server could allow malicious instructions into AI tools

item.title: Look What You Made Us Patch: 2025 Zero-Days in Review
item.description: Written by: Casey Charrier, James Sadowski, Zander Work, Clement Lecigne, Benoît Sevens, Fred Plan Executive Summary Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) tracked 90 zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in-the-wild in...

item.title: Coruna Exploit Kit Targets Older iPhones in Multi-Stage Campaigns
item.description: Exploit kit "Coruna" targets iPhones running iOS 13.0 to 17.2.1, focusing on financial data theft

item.title: CISA Adds Five Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2017-7921 Hikvision Multiple Products Improper Authentication Vulnerability...

item.title: UAT-9244 targets South American telecommunication providers with three new malware implants
item.description: Cisco Talos is disclosing UAT-9244, who we assess with high confidence is a China-nexus advanced persistent threat (APT) actor closely associated with Famous Sparrow.

item.title: Global Takedown Neutralizes Tycoon2FA Phishing Service
item.description: Law enforcers and industry partners have taken down notorious phishing-as-a-service platform Tycoon2FA
